chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Misc Application No.S-637 of 2025. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 3. For hearing of M.A No.7868/25. 20.10.2025. Mr. Shafqat Hussain Chandio, Advocate for Applicant. Syed Sarfraz Ali Shah, Advocate for Respondent No.3. Ms. Safa Hisbani, Assistant P.G for the State a/w SIP Muhammad Faisal Arain on behalf of SHO PS B-Section Latifabad, Hyderabad. ***************************************** When this lis was instituted in the High Court on 17.09.2025 against the impugned Order dated. 11.09.2025, passed by the Ex-Officio Justice of Peace, Hyderabad, no interim orders were obtained by the applicant/proposed accused to stay the investigation, and none are in force up to date. The Ex- Officio Justice of Peace impugned order left the matter of registration of FIR subject to the collection of final MCs of injured persons and further proceedings. According to the learned A.P.G., an FIR bearing No.200 of 2025 has been registered on 10.10.2025, and further investigation is underway. It appears that a new situation has arisen during the above-mentioned intervening period, with the MC now coming on record and the registration of the FIR. This Bench cannot intervene in the investigation arising out of the FIR. As a result of the above developments, this cr.misc.appln. has become infructuous. Meanwhile, I have read the impugned order dated 11.09.2025, which also directed the concerned police station to ensure the protection for all the parties, including the applicant/proposed accused. Counsel for the applicant/proposed accused contends that the respondents are causing a disturbance, preventing the children from attending school.

Meanwhile, I have read the impugned order dated 11.09.2025, which also directed the concerned police station to ensure the protection for all the parties, including the applicant/proposed accused. Counsel for the applicant/proposed accused contends that the respondents are causing a disturbance, preventing the children from attending school. The concerned SHO is once again directed to protect the parties in accordance with the law. The Cr. Misc Application stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE Ali.
